question: Does the output include a generated image of the cat converted into the Ghibli style?
Does the output include a second generated image depicting both the cat and the dog together in a single scene?
Does the output include a distinct text caption specifically suitable for social media at the end?
Does the output format follow an interleaved structure with at least two generated images and accompanying text blocks?
Does the generated Ghibli cat image clearly retain the visual identity of the source cat from [IMAGE1] (e.g., orange and white markings, specific pose)?
Does the generated cat image successfully mimic the art style of the reference dog image [IMAGE3] (e.g., soft shading, watercolor textures, anime-style line work)?
Does the composite scene image feature a "flower field" background as explicitly requested?
Does the composite scene depict the cat and dog actively playing or interacting, rather than just standing statically?
Is the dog in the generated composite scene recognizable as the same Golden Retriever character from [IMAGE3]?
Is the cat in the generated composite scene consistent in appearance (style and markings) with the standalone Ghibli cat generated earlier in the output?
Are the generated images free from significant AI artifacts (e.g., distorted faces, extra limbs, muddled anatomy)?
Does the generated caption adopt a "healing," heartwarming, or whimsical tone consistent with the requested Miyazaki vibe?
Does the social media caption accurately reflect the content of the composite image (e.g., mentioning the interaction or setting)?
Does the output follow the requested logical progression: Cat Conversion Image → Composite Scene Image → Caption?
Are the images inserted at logical breakpoints in the text (e.g., placing the conversion image after its description and before the composite scene)?
Does the composite scene successfully integrate the two characters into a unified environment without visible style inconsistencies or lighting mismatches between them?
